name: 5. 중학 접속사 200
description: 등위접속사(and/but/or/so), 시간/조건/이유/양보/목적/결과 종속접속사, that 명사절 예문 200개 (중학 수준)

words.w: I like apples and oranges.
She is smart and kind.
Tom and I went to school.
He sang and danced.
The cat and the dog play together.
I bought eggs and milk.
She studies hard and gets good grades.
words.m: [등위] 그녀는 열심히 공부하고 좋은 성적을 받는다. (and)

words.w: The room is small and cozy.
I'll go and help him.
The sun rose and the birds sang.
Open the door and come in.
He was tired and hungry.
We ate pizza and chicken.
Sit down and listen.
She smiled and waved.
I like coffee but not tea.
words.m: [등위] 나는 커피는 좋아하지만 차는 안 좋아한다. (but)

words.w: She is kind but strict.
He tried but failed.
The car is old but works well.
I called her but she didn't answer.
It was cold but sunny.
He is tall but thin.
I'm tired but happy.
She studied hard but got a low score.
words.m: [등위] 그녀는 열심히 공부했지만 낮은 점수를 받았다. (but)

words.w: The food was cheap but tasty.
Do you want tea or coffee?
Is it red or blue?
You can walk or take the bus.
Stay or leave, your choice.
Bring a pen or a pencil.
Friday or Saturday is fine.
Study hard, or you'll fail.
Is that her brother or her friend?
Hurry up, or we'll be late.
Do you prefer dogs or cats?
words.m: [등위] 개가 더 좋니, 고양이가 더 좋니? (or)

words.w: I was tired, so I slept.
It rained, so we stayed home.
She was hungry, so she ate.
He was late, so he ran.
I studied, so I passed.
When I was a child, I loved candy.
Call me when you arrive.
I was reading when she came in.
words.m: [시간] 그녀가 들어왔을 때 나는 책을 읽고 있었다. (when)

words.w: When it rains, I stay inside.
words.m: [시간] 비 올 때 나는 실내에 있는다. (when)

words.w: She smiled when she saw me.
When you leave, close the door.
I was happy when I got the gift.
He was sleeping when the phone rang.
words.m: [시간] 전화가 울렸을 때 그는 자고 있었다. (when)

words.w: She sang while she cooked.
I read while I wait.
He fell asleep while watching TV.
While I was walking, I saw a rainbow.
She listens to music while studying.
I wrote while he talked.
Be quiet while the baby sleeps.
Wait here until I come back.
I studied until midnight.
Stay until the rain stops.
She worked until late.
Don't leave until I tell you.
He waited until she arrived.
Keep trying until you succeed.
Brush your teeth before you sleep.
Wash your hands before you eat.
I finish homework before dinner.
Think before you speak.
She left before I arrived.
Call me before you come.
I'll pack before I leave.
words.m: [시간] 나는 떠나기 전에 짐을 쌀 것이다. (before)

words.w: I watch TV after dinner.
After he left, it rained.
She went home after the party.
We will meet after school.
He called after he arrived.
I felt tired after running.
Call me as soon as you arrive.
As soon as I saw him, I waved.
words.m: [시간] 그를 보자마자 손을 흔들었다. (as soon as)

words.w: He left as soon as he heard the news.
words.m: [시간] 그는 소식을 듣자마자 떠났다. (as soon as)

words.w: As soon as it rained, we ran inside.
words.m: [시간] 비가 오자마자 우리는 안으로 뛰었다. (as soon as)

words.w: She smiled as soon as she saw me.
words.m: [시간] 그녀는 나를 보자마자 웃었다. (as soon as)

words.w: If it rains, I'll stay home.
If you study, you'll pass.
I'll go if you come.
If she is tired, she rests.
Call me if you need help.
If I have time, I'll visit you.
If he is late, start without him.
If you win, I'll buy you dinner.
Don't eat it if it smells bad.
If you're hungry, have a snack.
If she calls, tell her I'm busy.
words.m: [조건] 그녀가 전화하면 내가 바쁘다고 말해 줘. (if)

words.w: If it's cold, wear a coat.
I'll help if I can.
I won't go unless you go.
words.m: [조건] 네가 가지 않으면 나도 안 간다. (unless = if not)

words.w: Unless you hurry, you'll miss the bus.
She won't come unless you invite her.
words.m: [조건] 네가 초대하지 않으면 그녀는 오지 않을 것이다. (unless)

words.w: Unless it rains, we'll play outside.
words.m: [조건] 비가 오지 않는 한 우리는 밖에서 놀 것이다. (unless)

words.w: You can't enter unless you have a ticket.
Unless you study, you'll fail.
I won't leave unless you come with me.
words.m: [조건] 네가 같이 가지 않으면 안 떠날 것이다. (unless)

words.w: I am tired because I worked hard.
She was happy because she passed.
Because it rained, we stayed inside.
He cried because he lost his dog.
I like her because she is kind.
We can't go because it's too late.
She is famous because of her talent.
words.m: [이유] 그녀는 재능 때문에 유명하다. (because of + 명사)

words.w: He failed because he didn't study.
I'm leaving because I'm tired.
She smiled because she was happy.
We won because we practiced hard.
He called because he missed us.
Since it was late, we went home.
Since you asked, I'll tell you.
Since he is sick, he can't come.
Since we have time, let's chat.
Since I don't know, I'll ask.
As she was tired, she slept early.
As it's cold, wear a jacket.
As you wish, I'll go.
Although it rained, we went out.
She came although she was tired.
Although he's young, he's wise.
I passed although the test was hard.
Although she is rich, she is humble.
We won although we were tired.
Although I'm busy, I'll help you.
Though it was cold, we swam.
She tried, though she was scared.
Though young, he is smart.
He came, though he was sick.
Though we lost, we had fun.
She smiled, though she was sad.
Though it's small, it's cozy.
Even though it's late, let's go.
She loves him even though he's poor.
words.m: [양보] 그가 가난해도 그녀는 그를 사랑한다. (even though)

words.w: I went even though I was tired.
Even though he's rich, he's lonely.
We won even though they were stronger.
words.m: [양보] 그들이 더 강했어도 우리는 이겼다. (even though)

words.w: She kept trying even though she failed.
I studied hard so that I could pass.
She saved money so that she could travel.
words.m: [목적] 여행하기 위해 그녀는 돈을 모았다. (so that)

words.w: He runs every day so that he can stay fit.
words.m: [목적] 건강을 유지하려고 그는 매일 달린다. (so that)

words.w: We left early so that we wouldn't be late.
words.m: [목적] 늦지 않으려고 우리는 일찍 떠났다. (so that)

words.w: Speak up so that I can hear you.
words.m: [목적] 내가 들을 수 있게 크게 말해라. (so that)

words.w: She wrote it down so that she would remember.
Take a map so that you won't get lost.
words.m: [목적] 길을 잃지 않도록 지도를 가져가라. (so that)

words.w: He works hard so that his family can live well.
words.m: [목적] 가족이 잘 살 수 있게 그는 열심히 일한다. (so that)

words.w: I called so that I could ask a question.
words.m: [목적] 질문을 할 수 있게 전화했다. (so that)

words.w: She studies so that she can become a doctor.
Be quiet so that the baby can sleep.
words.m: [목적] 아기가 잘 수 있게 조용히 해라. (so that)

words.w: He trains so that he can win.
I spoke slowly in order that they could understand.
words.m: [목적] 그들이 이해하도록 나는 천천히 말했다. (in order that)

words.w: He works hard in order that he can succeed.
words.m: [목적] 성공하도록 그는 열심히 일한다. (in order that)

words.w: We saved money in order that we could buy a house.
words.m: [목적] 집을 사기 위해 돈을 모았다. (in order that)

words.w: She studies in order that she can go to college.
words.m: [목적] 대학에 가기 위해 그녀는 공부한다. (in order that)

words.w: Turn on the light in order that we can see.
words.m: [목적] 우리가 볼 수 있도록 불을 켜라. (in order that)

words.w: He left a note in order that she would know.
words.m: [목적] 그녀가 알 수 있게 그는 메모를 남겼다. (in order that)

words.w: I took notes in order that I could remember.
We took a shortcut in order that we would arrive sooner.
words.m: [목적] 더 빨리 도착하려고 지름길로 갔다. (in order that)

words.w: It was so hot that I couldn't sleep.
words.m: [결과] 너무 더워서 잠을 잘 수 없었다. (so + 형용사 + that)

words.w: She was so tired that she fell asleep.
He ran so fast that no one could catch him.
words.m: [결과] 그는 너무 빨리 뛰어서 아무도 잡을 수 없었다. (so~that)

words.w: The box is so heavy that I can't lift it.
words.m: [결과] 상자가 너무 무거워서 들 수 없다. (so~that)

words.w: He is so kind that everyone loves him.
words.m: [결과] 그는 너무 친절해서 모두가 그를 좋아한다. (so~that)

words.w: It was so cold that the lake froze.
She was so happy that she cried.
The movie was so long that I got bored.
He spoke so quietly that I couldn't hear.
words.m: [결과] 그가 너무 조용히 말해서 듣지 못했다. (so~that)

words.w: It was so dark that I couldn't see.
She is so smart that she solves everything.
words.m: [결과] 그녀는 너무 똑똑해서 모든 것을 해결한다. (so~that)

words.w: The food was so spicy that I cried.
It was such a hot day that we stayed inside.
words.m: [결과] 너무 더운 날이어서 우리는 실내에 있었다. (such + a/an + 형용사 + 명사 + that)

words.w: She is such a kind person that everyone loves her.
words.m: [결과] 그녀는 너무 친절한 사람이어서 모두 그녀를 좋아한다. (such~that)

words.w: It was such a long movie that I fell asleep.
He is such a good teacher that students love him.
words.m: [결과] 그는 너무 좋은 선생님이어서 학생들이 그를 좋아한다. (such~that)

words.w: It was such cold weather that we wore coats.
words.m: [결과] 너무 추운 날씨여서 우리는 코트를 입었다. (such~that, 불가산)

words.w: She made such a big cake that we had leftovers.
words.m: [결과] 그녀가 너무 큰 케이크를 만들어서 남았다. (such~that)

words.w: He told such a funny joke that everyone laughed.
words.m: [결과] 그가 너무 재미있는 농담을 해서 모두 웃었다. (such~that)

words.w: It was such a beautiful day that we went for a walk.
I know that he is honest.
words.m: [that 명사절] 나는 그가 정직하다는 것을 안다. (목적어 역할)

words.w: I think that she is right.
She believes that he will come.
words.m: [that 명사절] 그녀는 그가 올 것이라고 믿는다.

words.w: It is true that he is smart.
words.m: [that 명사절] 그가 똑똑한 것은 사실이다. (가주어 It)

words.w: The fact that she won surprised us.
words.m: [that 명사절] 그녀가 이겼다는 사실이 우리를 놀라게 했다. (동격)

words.w: I hope that you feel better.
He said that he was tired.
We heard that it will rain.
words.m: [that 명사절] 우리는 비가 올 것이라고 들었다.

words.w: It is clear that she loves him.
words.m: [that 명사절] 그녀가 그를 사랑한다는 것은 분명하다.

words.w: I'm glad that you came.
She forgot that she had a meeting.
words.m: [that 명사절] 그녀는 회의가 있다는 것을 잊었다.

words.w: He told me that he was sorry.
I realize that I was wrong.
words.m: [that 명사절] 나는 내가 틀렸다는 것을 깨닫는다.

words.w: We agree that this is important.
words.m: [that 명사절] 우리는 이것이 중요하다는 데 동의한다.

words.w: I feel that something is wrong.
She admits that she made a mistake.
It seems that he is busy.
The news that he won spread fast.
words.m: [that 명사절] 그가 이겼다는 소식이 빠르게 퍼졌다. (동격)

words.w: I promise that I will come.
The idea that we can succeed is inspiring.
words.m: [that 명사절] 우리가 성공할 수 있다는 생각이 용기를 준다. (동격)
